test_not_tautological.reason: Since tests only check for string presence in source files (e.g., 'assert "already exists" in content'), an agent could create a file containing all required strings as comments or dead code without any functional implementation, and all fail-to-pass tests would pass. For example, a mcp.ts file with only comments containing the required strings would satisfy most tests.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution patch exists at /workspace/cline after solve.sh runs, and the test file test_outputs.py is accessible at the test path. An agent could read the solution file or grep for the exact fixture strings needed. Since tests only check for string presence, the agent could simply copy strings from test_outputs.py into source files to pass all assertions.
